hints.text: Plates: pre-2006 short **green**; post-2006 white (private) / **yellow** (commercial), long or short
Almost all **Gen3**; car back often visible (black/white/silver), **never an antenna**; lots of autumn/winter coverage
**Hangul** script — recognisable by the **circles** in many letters (unlike other East Asian scripts)
Utility poles: **black-yellow diagonal stripes** near the base (don't reach the bottom; Taiwan's do & are oranger); tapered **spike** on top
Drives **right**; yellow middle + white outer lines; occasional red block markings; yellow/white speed bumps
Triangular warning signs **yellow bg** (prohibition/yield = white); direction signs green w/ white text
Street signs **blue, arrow-shaped, hanging** from a bar on utility poles
Pedestrian-crossing signs = distinctive **pentagon shape, blue background**
Distinctive **house numbers**: blue, literally **house-shaped**, full address in Hangul + Latin
Wedge-shaped B&W bollards (round yellow reflector front, grey back)
Predominantly **mountainous** (almost always elevation on the horizon); red brick; traditional tiled roofs w/ upturned corners (blue roofs common)
Coverage spotty (cities + highways); **area codes increase south** (02 Seoul → 064 Jeju; Seoul = only 2-digit code)
**Jeju** = volcanic island: dark volcanic soil, **volcanic-rock walls**, volcanoes on the horizon; Gen2 → Seoul & Busan
